Now I have gathered sufficient information to create a comprehensive architecture documentation. Let me compile the findings into a detailed system architecture document. # ChronicCareAIPlatform - System Architecture Documentation ## 1. Executive Summary **ChronicCareAIPlatform** is a microservices-based chronic disease care management platform designed for healthcare organizations. The system enables patients to upload and manage health reports, doctors to manage schedules with AI-powered recommendations, and provides comprehensive messaging capabilities through multiple channels (SMS, email, in-app notifications). ### Key Architectural Characteristics | Characteristic | Implementation | |----------------|----------------| | **Architecture Style** | Microservices with Dubbo RPC | | **Runtime Framework** | Spring Boot 3.5.9, Java 17 | | **Service Communication** | Apache Dubbo 3.3.0 (RPC) | | **Service Discovery** | Alibaba Nacos 3.1.1 | | **API Gateway** | Spring Cloud Gateway | | **Authentication** | JWT (Dual-Token: Access + Refresh) | | **Authorization** | RBAC with AOP-based enforcement | | **Data Persistence** | MySQL with MyBatis-Plus | | **Caching** | Redis (tokens, sessions, permissions) | | **Rate Limiting** | Alibaba Sentinel | | **Cloud Integration** | Alibaba Cloud OSS | --- ## 2. System Architecture Overview ### 2.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "External Clients" WEB[Web Application] MOBILE[Mobile App] end subgraph "Infrastructure Layer" GW[API Gateway
Spring Cloud Gateway] NACOS[(Nacos
Service Registry)] SENTINEL[Sentinel
Rate Limiting] REDIS[(Redis
Cache/Sessions)] end subgraph "Authentication & Authorization" AUTH_CONSUMER[Auth Consumer
SSO Controller] AUTH_PROVIDER[Auth Provider
JWT Service] end subgraph "Core Business Services" ACC_PROVIDER[Account Provider
User & RBAC] HEALTH_CONSUMER[Health Report Consumer] HEALTH_PROVIDER[Health Report Provider] DOC_CONSUMER[Doctor Service Consumer] DOC_PROVIDER[Doctor Service Provider] end subgraph "Supporting Services" MSG_CONSUMER[Message Consumer] MSG_PROVIDER[Message Provider] THIRD_PROVIDER[Third-Party Provider
OSS/SMS/Email] end subgraph "External Systems" OSS[Alibaba Cloud OSS] SMS_GW[SMS Gateway] EMAIL_SVC[Email Service] MYSQL[(MySQL Database)] end WEB --> GW MOBILE --> GW GW --> NACOS GW --> SENTINEL GW --> AUTH_PROVIDER GW --> AUTH_CONSUMER GW --> HEALTH_CONSUMER GW --> DOC_CONSUMER GW --> MSG_CONSUMER AUTH_CONSUMER --> AUTH_PROVIDER AUTH_CONSUMER --> ACC_PROVIDER AUTH_PROVIDER --> ACC_PROVIDER AUTH_PROVIDER --> REDIS HEALTH_CONSUMER --> HEALTH_PROVIDER HEALTH_PROVIDER --> ACC_PROVIDER HEALTH_PROVIDER --> THIRD_PROVIDER DOC_CONSUMER --> DOC_PROVIDER MSG_CONSUMER --> MSG_PROVIDER MSG_PROVIDER --> ACC_PROVIDER MSG_PROVIDER --> THIRD_PROVIDER THIRD_PROVIDER --> OSS THIRD_PROVIDER --> SMS_GW THIRD_PROVIDER --> EMAIL_SVC ACC_PROVIDER --> MYSQL HEALTH_PROVIDER --> MYSQL DOC_PROVIDER --> MYSQL MSG_PROVIDER --> MYSQL ``` ### 2.2 Module Structure Diagram ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "API Contracts (-api modules)" ACC_API[account-api] AUTH_API[auth-api] HEALTH_API[health-center-api] DOC_API[doctor-service-api] MSG_API[message-system-api] THIRD_API[third-party-api] end subgraph "Consumer Layer (BFF)" AUTH_CON[auth-consumer] HEALTH_CON[health-center-consumer] DOC_CON[doctor-service-consumer] MSG_CON[message-system-consumer] end subgraph "Provider Layer (Business Logic)" ACC_PROV[account-provider] AUTH_PROV[auth-provider] HEALTH_PROV[health-center-provider] DOC_PROV[doctor-service-provider] MSG_PROV[message-system-provider] THIRD_PROV[third-party-provider] end subgraph "Shared Infrastructure" UTILS[utils] GATEWAY[gateway] end AUTH_CON --> AUTH_API AUTH_CON --> ACC_API AUTH_PROV --> AUTH_API HEALTH_CON --> HEALTH_API HEALTH_PROV --> HEALTH_API HEALTH_PROV --> ACC_API HEALTH_PROV --> THIRD_API DOC_CON --> DOC_API DOC_PROV --> DOC_API MSG_CON --> MSG_API MSG_PROV --> MSG_API MSG_PROV --> ACC_API MSG_PROV --> THIRD_API ACC_PROV --> ACC_API THIRD_PROV --> THIRD_API AUTH_CON --> UTILS HEALTH_CON --> UTILS DOC_CON --> UTILS GATEWAY --> AUTH_API ``` --- ## 3. Layered Architecture Pattern The platform implements a **three-tier module pattern** for each business domain: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Three-Tier Module Pattern" API["API Module (-api)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Dubbo Service Interfaces
• DTOs (Request/Response)
• Persistent Objects (PO)
• Value Objects (VO)
• Enumerations"] CONSUMER["Consumer Module (-consumer)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• REST Controllers
• Security Annotations
• Request Validation
• Response Formatting"] PROVIDER["Provider Module (-provider)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Dubbo Service Implementations
• Business Logic
• Data Access (Mappers)
• External Service Calls"] end CONSUMER -->|"Dubbo RPC"| PROVIDER CONSUMER -->|"implements"| API PROVIDER -->|"implements"| API ``` ### Benefits of This Pattern | Benefit | Description | |---------|-------------| | **Interface-Driven Development** | API modules define contracts that both consumers and providers implement | | **Loose Coupling** | Services communicate through well-defined interfaces | | **Independent Deployment** | Each module can be deployed and scaled independently | | **Type Safety** | Shared DTOs ensure type-safe RPC communication | | **Clear Separation** | HTTP handling (Consumer) separated from business logic (Provider) | --- ## 4. Authentication & Authorization Architecture ### 4.1 Dual-Token Authentication Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client participant Gateway participant AuthConsumer participant AccountProvider participant AuthProvider participant Redis Client->>Gateway: POST /auth/login Gateway->>AuthConsumer: Route (whitelisted) AuthConsumer->>AccountProvider: Dubbo: login(credentials) AccountProvider->>AccountProvider: Verify BCrypt password AccountProvider->>AccountProvider: Query roles & permissions AccountProvider-->>AuthConsumer: LoginResponse (userId, roles, permissions) AuthConsumer->>AuthProvider: Dubbo: generateToken(userId, roles, permissions) AuthProvider->>AuthProvider: Generate Access Token (30min) AuthProvider->>AuthProvider: Generate Refresh Token (7 days) AuthProvider->>Redis: Store refresh token AuthProvider-->>AuthConsumer: TokenPair (access + refresh) AuthConsumer-->>Gateway: LoginResult Gateway-->>Client: {accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn} ``` ### 4.2 Request Authorization Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client participant Gateway participant AuthProvider participant Redis participant Service participant AOP Client->>Gateway: GET /api/resource (Bearer token) Gateway->>Gateway: Extract Bearer token Gateway->>AuthProvider: Dubbo: validateToken(token) AuthProvider->>AuthProvider: Decode JWT AuthProvider->>Redis: Check blacklist AuthProvider-->>Gateway: ValidateTokenResponse (userId, roles, authorities) Gateway->>Gateway: Inject user headers Note over Gateway: X-User-Id, X-Username
X-User-Roles, X-User-Authorities Gateway->>Service: Forward request with headers Service->>Service: UserInfoExtractInterceptor Note over Service: Extract headers → ThreadLocal Service->>AOP: @RequireRole / @RequirePermission AOP->>AOP: PermissionCheckAspect AOP->>AOP: Validate against UserInfoManager alt Permission Granted Service-->>Gateway: Response Gateway-->>Client: 200 OK else Permission Denied AOP-->>Gateway: 403 Forbidden Gateway-->>Client: 403 Forbidden end ``` ### 4.3 JWT Token Structure ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Access Token (30 min)" AT_HEADER[Header
alg: RS256] AT_PAYLOAD[Payload
━━━━━━━━━━━━
sub: userId
type: access
username: string
roles: array
authorities: array
iat: timestamp
exp: timestamp] AT_SIG[Signature
RSA-SHA256] end subgraph "Refresh Token (7 days)" RT_HEADER[Header
alg: RS256] RT_PAYLOAD[Payload
━━━━━━━━━━━━
sub: userId
type: refresh
jti: tokenId
roles: array
iat: timestamp
exp: timestamp] RT_SIG[Signature
RSA-SHA256] end subgraph "Redis Storage" REFRESH_STORE["auth:refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"] BLACKLIST["auth:blacklist:{tokenId}"] PERMISSIONS["auth:permissions:{userId}"] end ``` ### 4.4 RBAC Permission Model ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Role Hierarchy" DOCTOR[DOCTOR
Code: 1] PATIENT[PATIENT
Code: 2] FAMILY[FAMILY
Code: 3] end subgraph "Actions" READ[READ
Code: 1] WRITE[WRITE
Code: 2] ALL[ALL
Code: 3] end subgraph "Permission Strings" P1["DOCTOR:READ"] P2["DOCTOR:WRITE"] P3["DOCTOR:ALL"] P4["PATIENT:READ"] P5["PATIENT:WRITE"] end DOCTOR --> P1 DOCTOR --> P2 DOCTOR --> P3 PATIENT --> P4 PATIENT --> P5 subgraph "Permission Expansion" ALL_EXP["ALL expands to:
READ + WRITE + ALL"] end ``` --- ## 5. Domain Module Architecture ### 5.1 Domain Relationship Diagram ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Infrastructure Domains" GW_DOMAIN[API Gateway Domain
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• JWT Validation
• Rate Limiting
• User Context Injection
• CORS Configuration] UTILS_DOMAIN[Common Utilities Domain
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• UserInfoManager
• Security Annotations
• Data Encryption
• XSS/SQL Filters] THIRD_DOMAIN[Third-Party Integration
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• OSS Service
• SMS Service
• Email Service] end subgraph "Core Business Domains" AUTH_DOMAIN[Authentication Domain
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• JWT Token Management
• Token Validation
• SSO Service] ACC_DOMAIN[Account Management
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• User Registration
• Login Verification
• Permission Service
• User Identity] HEALTH_DOMAIN[Health Report Center
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Report Upload
• Report Query
• Audit Workflow] DOC_DOMAIN[Doctor Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Schedule Management
• AI Recommendations
• Workbench APIs] end subgraph "Supporting Domains" MSG_DOMAIN[Message System
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Push Notifications
• SMS Delivery
• Email Delivery] end GW_DOMAIN -->|"Token Validation"| AUTH_DOMAIN GW_DOMAIN -->|"Permission Query"| ACC_DOMAIN AUTH_DOMAIN -->|"Credential Verification"| ACC_DOMAIN HEALTH_DOMAIN -->|"User Info"| ACC_DOMAIN HEALTH_DOMAIN -->|"File Upload"| THIRD_DOMAIN DOC_DOMAIN -->|"User Info"| ACC_DOMAIN MSG_DOMAIN -->|"User Info"| ACC_DOMAIN MSG_DOMAIN -->|"SMS/Email"| THIRD_DOMAIN AUTH_DOMAIN -.->|"Tool Support"| UTILS_DOMAIN ACC_DOMAIN -.->|"Tool Support"| UTILS_DOMAIN HEALTH_DOMAIN -.->|"Tool Support"| UTILS_DOMAIN DOC_DOMAIN -.->|"Tool Support"| UTILS_DOMAIN MSG_DOMAIN -.->|"Tool Support"| UTILS_DOMAIN ``` ### 5.2 Domain Importance Matrix | Domain | Type | Importance | Complexity | Key Responsibilities | |--------|------|------------|------------|---------------------| | **Authentication & Authorization** | Core | 9.5/10 | 8.5/10 | JWT lifecycle, token validation, SSO | | **Account Management** | Core | 9.0/10 | 7.5/10 | User CRUD, RBAC, identity management | | **Health Report Center** | Core | 9.0/10 | 7.0/10 | Report upload/query, audit workflow | | **API Gateway** | Infrastructure | 9.0/10 | 7.0/10 | Routing, auth filter, rate limiting | | **Doctor Service** | Core | 8.5/10 | 7.5/10 | Schedule management, AI recommendations | | **Common Utilities** | Tool Support | 8.5/10 | 6.0/10 | Security annotations, encryption, context | | **Message System** | Supporting | 7.5/10 | 6.5/10 | Multi-channel notifications | | **Third-Party Integration** | Infrastructure | 7.0/10 | 5.5/10 | OSS, SMS, Email adapters | --- ## 6. Key Business Flows ### 6.1 Health Report Upload Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Patient participant Gateway participant HealthConsumer participant HealthProvider participant AccountProvider participant OSSProvider participant MySQL Patient->>Gateway: POST /health/report/upload (file) Gateway->>Gateway: Validate JWT, inject user context Gateway->>HealthConsumer: Forward with X-User-* headers HealthConsumer->>HealthConsumer: @RequireRole("PATIENT") HealthConsumer->>HealthConsumer: Validate ownership HealthConsumer->>HealthProvider: Dubbo: uploadReport(request) HealthProvider->>AccountProvider: Dubbo: getPatientInfo(patientId) AccountProvider-->>HealthProvider: PatientVO (attendingDoctorId) HealthProvider->>OSSProvider: Dubbo: uploadFile(file) OSSProvider->>OSSProvider: AliOSSUtils.uploadFile() OSSProvider-->>HealthProvider: fileUrl HealthProvider->>MySQL: INSERT health_report HealthProvider-->>HealthConsumer: UploadReportResponse HealthConsumer-->>Gateway: Response Gateway-->>Patient: {reportId, fileUrl} ``` ### 6.2 Doctor Schedule Management Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Doctor participant Gateway participant DoctorConsumer participant DoctorProvider participant MySQL Doctor->>Gateway: GET /doctor/workbench/schedule Gateway->>Gateway: Validate JWT Gateway->>DoctorConsumer: Forward with user context DoctorConsumer->>DoctorConsumer: @RequireRole("DOCTOR") DoctorConsumer->>DoctorConsumer: @RequirePermission("DOCTOR:READ") DoctorConsumer->>DoctorProvider: Dubbo: querySchedule(doctorId, filters) DoctorProvider->>DoctorProvider: Validate doctorId ownership DoctorProvider->>MySQL: SELECT doctor_schedule DoctorProvider->>DoctorProvider: Convert to ScheduleVO DoctorProvider-->>DoctorConsumer: QueryScheduleResponse DoctorConsumer-->>Gateway: Response Gateway-->>Doctor: {schedules, total} ``` --- ## 7. Security Architecture ### 7.1 Security Layers ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Layer 1: Gateway Security" L1_1[JWT Token Validation] L1_2[Token Blacklist Check] L1_3[Rate Limiting - Sentinel] L1_4[CORS Configuration] end subgraph "Layer 2: Application Security" L2_1["@RequireRole Annotation"] L2_2["@RequirePermission Annotation"] L2_3[PermissionCheckAspect - AOP] L2_4[UserInfoManager - ThreadLocal] end subgraph "Layer 3: Data Security" L3_1[BCrypt Password Hashing] L3_2[AES-CBC Field Encryption] L3_3[SHA-256 Hash for Lookups] L3_4[XSS/SQL Injection Filters] end subgraph "Layer 4: Business Security" L4_1[Resource Ownership Validation] L4_2[State Machine Transitions] L4_3[Optimistic Locking] L4_4[Audit Logging] end L1_1 --> L2_1 L2_1 --> L3_1 L3_1 --> L4_1 ``` ### 7.2 Sensitive Data Encryption ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Write Path" PLAIN[Plain Text
phone: 13812345678] ENCRYPT[AES-CBC Encrypt] BASE64[Base64 Encode] DB_WRITE[(MySQL
encrypted value)] end subgraph "Read Path" DB_READ[(MySQL
encrypted value)] DECODE[Base64 Decode] DECRYPT[AES-CBC Decrypt] RESULT[Plain Text
phone: 13812345678] end PLAIN --> ENCRYPT --> BASE64 --> DB_WRITE DB_READ --> DECODE --> DECRYPT --> RESULT subgraph "MyBatis TypeHandler" HANDLER[SensitiveDataEncryptHandler
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Algorithm: AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding
Key: 16 bytes
IV: 16 bytes] end ``` --- ## 8. Infrastructure Components ### 8.1 API Gateway Configuration ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Gateway Components" FILTER[TraceAndJwtAuthFilter
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Generate/propagate TraceId
• Validate JWT via Dubbo
• Inject user headers
• Whitelist management] SENTINEL_CFG[SentinelGatewayConfig
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Flow rules from Nacos
• Degrade rules
• Custom block handler] CORS[CorsConfiguration
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Allowed origins
• Allowed methods
• Exposed headers] ROUTES[Route Configuration
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• /auth/** → auth-provider
• /account/** → account-service
• /health/** → health-service
• /doctor/** → doctor-service] end subgraph "Whitelist Paths" WL1["/auth/login"] WL2["/auth/register"] WL3["/auth/refresh"] WL4["/actuator/**"] end ``` ### 8.2 User Context Propagation ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Gateway participant Service participant Interceptor participant ThreadLocal participant Controller participant AOP Gateway->>Service: HTTP Request with headers Note over Gateway,Service: X-User-Id: 123
X-Username: john
X-User-Roles: DOCTOR
X-User-Authorities: DOCTOR:READ,DOCTOR:WRITE Service->>Interceptor: preHandle() Interceptor->>Interceptor: Extract headers Interceptor->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.setUserContext() Interceptor->>Controller: Continue request Controller->>AOP: Method with @RequireRole AOP->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.getRoles() AOP->>AOP: Validate permissions Controller->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.getUserId() Note over Controller: Business logic uses context Controller-->>Interceptor: Response Interceptor->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.clearUserContext() Note over Interceptor: Prevent memory leak ``` --- ## 9. Data Architecture ### 9.1 Database Schema Overview ```mermaid erDiagram ACCOUNT ||--o{ ACCOUNT_ROLE : has ACCOUNT_ROLE }o--|| ROLE : references ROLE ||--o{ ROLE_PERMISSION : has ROLE_PERMISSION }o--|| PERMISSION : references ACCOUNT ||--o| DOCTOR : extends ACCOUNT ||--o| PATIENT : extends PATIENT ||--o{ HEALTH_REPORT : uploads DOCTOR ||--o{ HEALTH_REPORT : reviews DOCTOR ||--o{ DOCTOR_SCHEDULE : manages ACCOUNT ||--o{ MESSAGE : sends ACCOUNT ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : receives MESSAGE ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : has ACCOUNT { bigint account_id PK varchar username UK varchar password varchar phone "encrypted" varchar phone_hash UK varchar id_card "encrypted" varchar id_card_hash UK varchar email varchar nickname int gender date birthday varchar avatar_url varchar address json ext } DOCTOR { bigint doctor_id PK bigint account_id FK varchar license_number varchar specialty bigint department_id varchar title } PATIENT { bigint patient_id PK bigint account_id FK bigint attending_doctor_id FK varchar medical_record_no text medical_history } HEALTH_REPORT { bigint report_id PK bigint patient_id FK bigint attending_doctor_id FK int report_type varchar category varchar title text description varchar file_url text text_content date report_date int status bigint uploader_id } DOCTOR_SCHEDULE { bigint schedule_id PK bigint doctor_id FK bigint patient_id FK date schedule_day time start_time time end_time int schedule_category int priority int status text diagnostic_report text prescription text notes } ``` --- ## 10. Technology Stack Summary ### 10.1 Core Technologies | Category | Technology | Version | Purpose | |----------|------------|---------|---------| | **Runtime** | Java | 17 | Language runtime | | **Framework** | Spring Boot | 3.5.9 | Application framework | | **RPC** | Apache Dubbo | 3.3.0 | Service communication | | **Gateway** | Spring Cloud Gateway | 2025.0.1 | API gateway | | **Service Discovery** | Alibaba Nacos | 3.1.1 | Registry & config | | **Rate Limiting** | Alibaba Sentinel | - | Flow control | | **Database** | MySQL | - | Primary data store | | **ORM** | MyBatis-Plus | - | Data access | | **Cache** | Redis | - | Sessions, tokens, cache | | **Security** | Spring Security | - | Authentication framework | | **JWT** | Nimbus JOSE+JWT | - | Token handling | ### 10.2 External Integrations | Service | Provider | Purpose | |---------|----------|---------| | **Object Storage** | Alibaba Cloud OSS | Health report files | | **SMS Gateway** | Third-party | Notifications, verification | | **Email Service** | SMTP/API | Email notifications | --- ## 11. Architectural Validation ### 11.1 Implementation vs Documentation Alignment | Documented Pattern | Implementation Status | Evidence | |--------------------|----------------------|----------| | Three-tier module pattern (API/Consumer/Provider) | ✅ Fully Implemented | All business modules follow this pattern | | Dual-token authentication | ✅ Fully Implemented | `JwtUtils.generateTokenPair()`, Redis storage | | RBAC with AOP enforcement | ✅ Fully Implemented | `@RequireRole`, `@RequirePermission`, `PermissionCheckAspect` | | User context propagation via headers | ✅ Fully Implemented | `TraceAndJwtAuthFilter`, `UserInfoExtractInterceptor` | | Sensitive data encryption | ✅ Fully Implemented | `SensitiveDataEncryptHandler` with AES-CBC | | Gateway-based JWT validation | ✅ Fully Implemented | `TraceAndJwtAuthFilter` calls `TokenValidationAPI` | | Dubbo RPC for inter-service communication | ✅ Fully Implemented | `@DubboService`, `@DubboReference` annotations | ### 11.2 Identified Gaps | Gap | Description | Recommendation | |-----|-------------|----------------| | **AI Integration** | Doctor schedule AI recommendations are mocked | Implement actual AI/ML service integration | | **Async Messaging** | No message queue for async operations | Consider adding RocketMQ/Kafka for event-driven patterns | | **Distributed Tracing** | TraceId propagation exists but no full tracing | Integrate with SkyWalking or Zipkin | | **API Documentation** | No OpenAPI/Swagger documentation found | Add springdoc-openapi for API documentation | | **Health Checks** | Basic actuator endpoints only | Implement comprehensive health indicators | --- ## 12. Deployment Architecture ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Load Balancer" LB[Nginx / Cloud LB] end subgraph "Gateway Cluster" GW1[Gateway Instance 1] GW2[Gateway Instance 2] end subgraph "Service Cluster" subgraph "Auth Services" AUTH1[Auth Provider 1] AUTH2[Auth Provider 2] end subgraph "Account Services" ACC1[Account Provider 1] ACC2[Account Provider 2] end subgraph "Business Services" HEALTH1[Health Provider 1] DOC1[Doctor Provider 1] MSG1[Message Provider 1] end end subgraph "Infrastructure" NACOS_CLUSTER[Nacos Cluster] REDIS_CLUSTER[Redis Cluster] MYSQL_CLUSTER[MySQL Master-Slave] end LB --> GW1 LB --> GW2 GW1 --> NACOS_CLUSTER GW2 --> NACOS_CLUSTER AUTH1 --> NACOS_CLUSTER AUTH2 --> NACOS_CLUSTER ACC1 --> NACOS_CLUSTER ACC2 --> NACOS_CLUSTER AUTH1 --> REDIS_CLUSTER AUTH2 --> REDIS_CLUSTER ACC1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER ACC2 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER HEALTH1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER DOC1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER MSG1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER ``` --- ## 13. Conclusion The **ChronicCareAIPlatform** demonstrates a well-architected microservices system with: 1. **Clear Separation of Concerns**: The three-tier module pattern (API/Consumer/Provider) ensures clean boundaries between HTTP handling, business logic, and data access. 2. **Robust Security**: Multi-layered security with JWT authentication, RBAC authorization, AOP-based permission enforcement, and data encryption at rest. 3. **Scalable Design**: Dubbo RPC with Nacos service discovery enables horizontal scaling of individual services. 4. **Maintainable Codebase**: Shared utilities module provides consistent patterns for user context, security annotations, and exception handling. 5. **Production-Ready Infrastructure**: Integration with Sentinel for rate limiting, Redis for caching/sessions, and cloud services for storage and messaging. The architecture aligns well with the documented design patterns and provides a solid foundation for a healthcare management platform serving patients, doctors, and administrators.
